channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Keeping children's data safe: How Xplor education is raising the bar on technology security
item.description: As cyber threats to the education sector grow more sophisticated, Xplor‘s Chief Security Officer Kevin Kanji explains why we consider robust technology safety as a fundamental obligation to families and educators alike.
item.content:encoded: As cyber threats to the education sector grow more sophisticated, Xplor‘s Chief Security Officer Kevin Kanji explains why we consider robust technology safety as a fundamental obligation to families and educators alike.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: MindChamps founder David Chiem to receive honorary doctorate from Western Sydney University
item.description: MindChamps founder CEO and Executive Chairman David Chiem has been awarded a Doctor of Letters (honoris causa) by Western Sydney University (WSU), recognising his contribution to early childhood education, literacy and educational innovation in Australia and internationally.
item.content:encoded: MindChamps founder CEO and Executive Chairman David Chiem has been awarded a Doctor of Letters (honoris causa) by Western Sydney University (WSU), recognising his contribution to early childhood education, literacy and educational innovation in Australia and internationally.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Abuse survivors’ legal fight after serial childcare paedophile unmasked 30 years on - Nine.com.au
item.description: Abuse survivors’ legal fight after serial childcare paedophile unmasked 30 years on - Nine.com.au
item.content:encoded: Abuse survivors’ legal fight after serial childcare paedophile unmasked 30 years on - Nine.com.au
item.dc:creator: Nine.com.au

item.title: NSW turns up the heat on early learning services as compliance activity surges
item.description: New figures from the NSW Early Learning Commission reveal a significant increase in regulatory activity since the independent regulator commenced operations in December 2025, signalling a more interventionist approach to compliance and enforcement across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.content:encoded: New figures from the NSW Early Learning Commission reveal a significant increase in regulatory activity since the independent regulator commenced operations in December 2025, signalling a more interventionist approach to compliance and enforcement across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: What the rating doesn’t tell parents: the gap between the NQS and a confident childcare decision
item.description: Ask any parent how they chose their child’s early learning service, and you will rarely hear them cite a National Quality Standard (NQS) rating. They will talk about the warmth of the educators, whether their questions were answered honestly, how the room felt during the tour, and whether the centre kept its word about the small things. That instinct is assessing the things that matter. It is also a quiet indictment of how little the formal system actually tells families when they need it most.
item.content:encoded: Ask any parent how they chose their child’s early learning service, and you will rarely hear them cite a National Quality Standard (NQS) rating. They will talk about the warmth of the educators, whether their questions were answered honestly, how the room felt during the tour, and whether the centre kept its word about the small things. That instinct is assessing the things that matter. It is also a quiet indictment of how little the formal system actually tells families when they need it most.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Elevating child safety & educator well being
item.description: The early childhood education sector is trusted with the care of young children every day, making child safety, compliance and educator wellbeing essential.
item.content:encoded: The early childhood education sector is trusted with the care of young children every day, making child safety, compliance and educator wellbeing essential.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: How active play supports child safety
item.description: With national child safety reforms landing, services are having to re-examine daily practice. Here’s why educator-led energetic play is one of your strongest safeguards under QA2.
item.content:encoded: With national child safety reforms landing, services are having to re-examine daily practice. Here’s why educator-led energetic play is one of your strongest safeguards under QA2.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Leadership Calls for Real Action on Child Safety

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
